title: WebGPU bloom upstream-block confirmed

stories.title: **WEBGPU-shadermaterial-stacktrace** ÔÇö re-enabled the S65 bloom path under controlled probe + patched `console.error` via playwrightÔÇŽ
stories.summary: **WEBGPU-shadermaterial-stacktrace** ÔÇö re-enabled the S65 bloom path under controlled probe + patched `console.error` via playwright `page.addInitScript` to capture stacks. Five identical stacks during first frame, all from `WebGPURenderer._renderObjectDirect` Ôćĺ `NodeManager.needsRefresh` Ôćĺ `RenderObject.getMonitor` / `getNodeBuilderState` Ôćĺ `WGSLNodeBuilder.build` Ôćĺ `prebuild` Ôćĺ `error()`. Confirms the offending `RenderObject` is a Mesh in the rendered draw list. Source: three.js's internal bloom orchestration (BloomNode's pingpong quads use vanilla `ShaderMaterial`).
verification: legacy import ÔÇö see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **Bloom revert (clean)** ÔÇö reverted the temporary bloom code; webgpu-spike preserved working (env=generated, probe, castShadow).
verification: legacy import ÔÇö see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **Research doc update** ÔÇö `docs/research/m21-webgpu-shadermaterial-audit.md` extended with the stack trace + verdict.

followUps: **S68 ÔÇö migrate post-processing-free projects to WebGPU** (hello-3d, physics-bench, batch-bench, webgpu-light-test as a sanity baseline). Confirms the WebGPU core path is mature enough for real migrations on projects that don't need post-passes / CSM / planar mirror.
**Periodic three.js minor check** ÔÇö every minor release, re-run the bloom test; flip `supportsPostProcessing: true` once upstream fixes the ShaderMaterial issue.
notes: Deliverables: `docs/research/m21-webgpu-shadermaterial-audit.md` extended with stack trace + verdict.; Bloom code reverted cleanly. `supportsPostProcessing: false` on WebGPU stays.
Verification: typecheck + 511 unit tests pass.; `engine:check` clean on 9 projects.; webgpu-spike: `renderer=webgpu`, 4 meshes, 3 draws, scene renders, zero pageerrors.

Verdict: Pin a different three.js version once a working post-processing path lands (likely r0.185+).; Fork `BloomNode` to use `NodeMaterial` for internal quads (large maintenance burden, not recommended).; Park WebGPU post-processing entirely until upstream publishes the fix.
